analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-283

POL-283.title: IMPLEMENT: Complete cognition librarian lifecycle wiring and adaptive coverage policy
POL-283.body:
## Objective

Complete the integration plumbing for the cognition librarian lifecycle that was specified but not wired during the [POL-249](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-249/implement-define-and-enforce-folder-local-work-note-staging-and) cluster, and add the adaptive folder coverage policy identified by [POL-200](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-200/analyze-adaptive-folder-cognition-and-librarian-role-lifecycle) analysis.

## Context

[POL-249](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-249/implement-define-and-enforce-folder-local-work-note-staging-and) and its children ([POL-250](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-250/implement-create-polariscognition-staging-folder-structure-and)–255) are marked Done in Linear. [POL-254](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-254/implement-implement-foreman-cognition-librarian-dispatch-and-patch) successfully delivered `src/cognition/librarian-dispatch.ts` and `src/cognition/librarian-types.ts` with full dispatch/validation logic. [POL-255](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-255/implement-archive-model-cognition-index-and-provenance-records-for) delivered `src/cognition/archive.ts`. However, git forensics during [POL-200](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-200/analyze-adaptive-folder-cognition-and-librarian-role-lifecycle) analysis revealed that several child commits were empty or partial:

* [POL-252](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-252/implement-extend-compactreturn-to-include-work-note-path-and-validate) commit was empty — `CompactReturn` was never extended with `work_note_paths`
* [POL-251](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-251/implement-define-worker-note-schema-and-update-worker-packet-to) commit did not modify `worker.ts` — workers still use the deprecated direct-write pattern
* [POL-250](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-250/implement-create-polariscognition-staging-folder-structure-and) commit did not create `.polaris/cognition/` directory structure
* [POL-254](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-254/implement-implement-foreman-cognition-librarian-dispatch-and-patch) committed the dispatch module but did NOT wire `dispatchCognitionLibrarian` into `loop/continue.ts`

The dispatch module is ready; the integration plumbing is not.

## Source Analysis

* Analysis issue: [POL-200](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-200/analyze-adaptive-folder-cognition-and-librarian-role-lifecycle)
* Analysis artifact: `smartdocs/specs/active/pol-200-adaptive-folder-cognition-librarian-lifecycle.md`
* Spec: `smartdocs/specs/active/folder-cognition-staging-librarian.md`

## Goal

Deliver the staged cognition model end to end: workers write notes, `loop continue` dispatches the librarian per folder, patches are validated and applied, notes are archived. Add the adaptive coverage policy that defines which folders proactively receive cognition files.

## Scope

* Create `.polaris/cognition/` staging directory structure with gitignore entry
* Extend `CompactReturn` with `work_note_paths?: string[]`
* Update `src/loop/worker.ts` to write cognition notes before emitting CompactReturn
* Wire `dispatchCognitionLibrarian` into `src/loop/continue.ts` after each child completes (non-blocking)
* Define and implement the adaptive folder coverage policy (3-tier model from [POL-200](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-200/analyze-adaptive-folder-cognition-and-librarian-role-lifecycle) analysis §1.3)
* Update `.polaris/roles/librarian.md` to distinguish SmartDocs Librarian from Cognition Librarian

## Acceptance Criteria

* Workers write staged notes to `.polaris/cognition/pending/<folder-slug>/` before emitting CompactReturn
* `CompactReturn` includes `work_note_paths` and the foreman collects them
* `loop continue` calls `dispatchCognitionLibrarian` after each child and emits telemetry
* Cognition librarian failures do not halt cluster execution
* Archive and provenance records are written for reconciled notes
* Adaptive coverage policy is documented and enforced

## Validation

* `npm run build`
* `npm test`
* Verify the delivered child set composes into the end-to-end staged cognition reconciliation flow

## Ordering

Execute children in dependency order as defined in the cluster plan.

## Non-goals

* Do not allow direct worker mutation of `POLARIS.md` or `SUMMARY.md` (new children — existing code not changed yet)
* Do not re-implement `librarian-dispatch.ts`, `librarian-types.ts`, or `archive.ts` — they are complete
* Do not require cognition files in every repo folder

POL-255.title: IMPLEMENT: Archive model, cognition-index, and provenance records for reconciled notes

POL-254.title: IMPLEMENT: Implement foreman cognition-librarian dispatch and patch validation

POL-200.title: ANALYZE: Adaptive folder cognition and Librarian role lifecycle

POL-252.title: IMPLEMENT: Extend CompactReturn to include work_note_path and validate note presence

POL-251.title: IMPLEMENT: Define worker note schema and update worker packet to require note writing

POL-250.title: IMPLEMENT: Create .polaris/cognition/ staging folder structure and POLARIS.md contract

POL-249.title: IMPLEMENT: Define and enforce folder-local work note staging and cognition librarian reconciliation model

POL-287.title: IMPLEMENT: Define and implement adaptive folder cognition coverage policy
POL-287.body:
## Objective

Implement the adaptive folder cognition coverage policy from [POL-200](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-200/analyze-adaptive-folder-cognition-and-librarian-role-lifecycle) analysis §1.3, defining which folders should proactively receive `POLARIS.md` / `SUMMARY.md` and protecting user-created cognition files.

## Context

The current `isCognitionSkippedFolder()` is reactive — it decides whether to skip a folder when a change has already been detected. There is no proactive policy for which folders should receive cognition files. The [POL-200](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-200/analyze-adaptive-folder-cognition-and-librarian-role-lifecycle) analysis (§1.3) defines a 3-tier model.

## Goal

Implement the 3-tier coverage policy, the coverage floor, and the user-protection guard so the cognition system is low-noise and non-destructive.

## Scope

### Tier 1 — Polaris-owned folders (always get cognition)

Define a `POLARIS_OWNED_COGNITION_FOLDERS` constant listing folders that always receive `POLARIS.md`:

* `.polaris/`
* `src/` and each immediate `src/<subdirectory>/`
* `smartdocs/specs/active/`
* `smartdocs/doctrine/active/` (when it exists)

Expose a `isPolarisOwnedFolder(folderRel: string): boolean` function from `src/cognition/route-cognition-delta.ts`.

### Tier 2 — Adaptive signal-based folders

A folder is eligible for cognition file creation when ALL of:

1. Contains ≥1 non-test, non-generated source file
2. `detectOperationalReasons(touchedFiles)` returns non-empty for files in that folder
3. `isCognitionSkippedFolder()` returns false

No change needed to existing logic — document this as the definition of "eligible" in POLARIS.md.

### Tier 3 — User-protection guard

Implement `isUserCreatedCognitionSurface(filePath: string, repoRoot: string): boolean`:

* Returns true if the file predates Polaris initialization (compare mtime vs. `.polaris/` creation date)
* OR if the file appears in a Polaris-managed surfaces manifest at `.polaris/cognition/managed-surfaces.json`

Update `applyRouteCognitionDelta` and `applySummaryDelta` to check `isUserCreatedCognitionSurface` before writing. If user-created, skip and emit a `cognition-user-surface-protected` telemetry event instead.

### Coverage floor

Document in `src/cognition/POLARIS.md`:

* Never create cognition in folders containing ONLY test files, generated files, or hidden config
* This is already enforced by `isCognitionSkippedFolder()` — document it explicitly

## Validation

* `npm run build`
* `npm test`
* Unit tests for `isPolarisOwnedFolder` and `isUserCreatedCognitionSurface`
* Verify `applyRouteCognitionDelta` skips a user-created surface and emits telemetry

## Acceptance Criteria

* `isPolarisOwnedFolder` correctly identifies Polaris-owned folders
* `isUserCreatedCognitionSurface` correctly identifies pre-existing files
* Worker cannot overwrite a user-created `POLARIS.md` or `SUMMARY.md` without explicit operator approval
* Coverage policy is documented in `src/cognition/POLARIS.md`

## Non-goals

* Do not require every repo folder to carry cognition files
* Do not introduce hosted Polaris APIs

POL-286.title: IMPLEMENT: Wire dispatchCognitionLibrarian into loop continue after each child
POL-286.body:
## Objective

Wire `dispatchCognitionLibrarian` into `src/loop/continue.ts` so cognition reconciliation runs after every child completes — before the next child's bootstrap packet is generated.

## Context

[POL-254](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-254/implement-implement-foreman-cognition-librarian-dispatch-and-patch) delivered the full `dispatchCognitionLibrarian` + `validateAndApplyLibrarianResult` implementation in `src/cognition/librarian-dispatch.ts`. However, it was never called from the run loop. The function is exported but unreachable. [POL-285](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-285/implement-extend-compactreturn-with-work-note-paths-and-update-worker) will add `work_note_paths` to CompactReturn. This child reads those paths from the foreman's state and dispatches the librarian.

## Goal

Close the final integration gap: foreman collects `work_note_paths`, dispatches the cognition librarian per folder, validates and applies patches, archives notes.

## Scope

### `src/loop/continue.ts`

After bridging completion evidence to cluster-state and before generating the bootstrap packet for the next child:

1. Read `work_note_paths` from the CompactReturn result (already parsed in the evidence verification step)
2. If `work_note_paths` is non-empty, call `await dispatchCognitionLibrarian({ runId, clusterId, workNotePaths, repoRoot, adapter, provider, telemetryFile })`
3. Log outcomes per folder to telemetry (already emitted by `dispatchCognitionLibrarian` internals)
4. Continue regardless of outcome — librarian failure is NON-BLOCKING

### Telemetry

The `cognition-librarian-dispatched`, `cognition-librarian-result-received`, `cognition-librarian-patch-applied`, and `cognition-librarian-patch-rejected` events are already emitted by `dispatchCognitionLibrarian`. No additional telemetry wiring needed.

### Adapter wiring

`dispatchCognitionLibrarian` requires an `ExecutionAdapter` instance. Use the same adapter that `loop continue` uses for the next child dispatch. The adapter is resolved from config (already available in the continue flow).

## Validation

* `npm run build`
* `npm test`
* Integration test: simulate a worker that writes a pending note and verify `loop continue` dispatches the librarian and archives the note

## Acceptance Criteria

* `loop continue` calls `dispatchCognitionLibrarian` when `work_note_paths` is non-empty
* A librarian dispatch failure does not halt cluster execution (error is logged to telemetry only)
* Notes with `docs_impact: none` are skipped (already handled by `dispatchCognitionLibrarian` internals)
* Telemetry events are present in the run's `telemetry.jsonl`

## Non-goals

* Do not deprecate `applyRouteCognitionDelta` direct-write in this child — phase-out is separate
* Do not require librarian success for `loop continue` to advance

POL-285.title: IMPLEMENT: Extend CompactReturn with work_note_paths and update worker to write cognition notes
POL-285.body:
## Objective

Add `work_note_paths` to `CompactReturn` and update `src/loop/worker.ts` so workers write a folder-scoped cognition note before emitting CompactReturn.

## Context

[POL-251](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-251/implement-define-worker-note-schema-and-update-worker-packet-to) and [POL-252](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-252/implement-extend-compactreturn-to-include-work-note-path-and-validate) were marked Done but their commits were empty. The `CompactReturn` schema still lacks `work_note_paths`, and `worker.ts` still calls `applyRouteCognitionDelta` / `applySummaryDelta` directly (deprecated direct-write pattern). The dispatch module (`librarian-dispatch.ts`) expects `work_note_paths` from the CompactReturn to know which notes to reconcile.

## Goal

Close the worker → staging gap: workers declare their work notes in CompactReturn; the foreman can read those paths in `loop continue`.

## Scope

### `src/loop/compact-return.ts`

* Add `work_note_paths?: string[]` — optional array of repo-relative paths to pending notes written by the worker
* Update `validateCompactReturn` to accept (not require) the field — missing is valid (not all children produce notes)

### `src/loop/worker.ts`

* After implementation work and before emitting CompactReturn, write one work note to `.polaris/cognition/pending/<folder-slug>/<run-id>-<child-id>.md`
* Determine `folder_slug` from primary affected folder (most-changed files)
* Set `docs_impact` based on whether `applyRouteCognitionDelta` / `applySummaryDelta` would warrant update
* Include required frontmatter fields per spec §2.2
* Keep prose body ≤150 words
* Emit `work_note_paths: [<path>]` in CompactReturn

### `.polaris/roles/librarian.md`

* Add section distinguishing SmartDocs Librarian from Cognition Librarian
* Scope SmartDocs Librarian to `smartdocs/` only; reference cognition-librarian role for folder cognition reconciliation

## Validation

* `npm run build`
* `npm test`
* Verify an integration test or smoke test shows a note written to `.polaris/cognition/pending/` after a worker completes

## Acceptance Criteria

* `CompactReturn` schema includes `work_note_paths` (optional)
* `worker.ts` writes a valid note before emitting CompactReturn
* `.polaris/roles/librarian.md` clearly distinguishes the two librarian concepts

## Non-goals

* Do not wire `dispatchCognitionLibrarian` into `loop continue` here — that is the next child (POL-286)
* Do not deprecate `applyRouteCognitionDelta` yet — phase-out is after the full loop is wired

POL-284.title: IMPLEMENT: Create .polaris/cognition/ staging directory structure and gitignore entry
POL-284.body:
## Objective

Create the `.polaris/cognition/` staging directory structure that worker note writing and cognition librarian dispatch depend on.

## Context

[POL-250](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-250/implement-create-polariscognition-staging-folder-structure-and) was marked Done but its commit did not create the `.polaris/cognition/` directory. The directory, subdirectories, placeholder files, and gitignore entry are all missing. Nothing can proceed without this foundation.

## Goal

Create the staging root, pending and archive subdirectories, the POLARIS.md contract, and the gitignore entry for ephemeral pending notes.

## Scope

* Create `.polaris/cognition/pending/.gitkeep`
* Create `.polaris/cognition/archive/.gitkeep`
* Create `.polaris/cognition/POLARIS.md` (staging root contract — defines worker note placement and librarian access scope)
* Add `.polaris/cognition/pending/` to `.gitignore` (ephemeral staging, not committed)
* Verify archive/ is tracked (no gitignore entry)

## Validation

* `npm run build`
* `npm test`
* verify `.polaris/cognition/pending/` is gitignored
* verify `.polaris/cognition/archive/` is tracked

## Acceptance Criteria

* `.polaris/cognition/` directory structure exists as specified in `smartdocs/specs/active/folder-cognition-staging-librarian.md` §1

## Non-goals

* Do not implement worker note writing here — that is [POL-283](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-283/implement-complete-cognition-librarian-lifecycle-wiring-and-adaptive)-B
* Do not create any pending notes manually
